# Medical Safety Boundary

NALA-DNA-Med must keep a strict public boundary.

## Allowed

- research-support workflows
- source organization
- literature review assistance
- hypothesis tracking
- local AI experimentation
- project notes
- safety and risk comparison prompts
- exportable support reports

## Not Allowed

- diagnosis
- treatment instructions
- prescription advice
- emergency medical guidance
- claims that the app can cure a disease
- claims that a generated compound, pathway, or idea is effective without validation
- replacing a doctor, researcher, lab, ethics board, regulator, or clinical trial

## Required Language

Use this boundary in the app and public docs:

> NALA-DNA-Med is research-support software. It is not medical advice, not a medical device, and not validated for diagnosis, treatment, prescription, or clinical decision-making.

## Why This Boundary Matters

The project can be ambitious without making unsafe claims. Clear language protects users, contributors, researchers, and the credibility of the project.
